page: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores, utilizing the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10%. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations ranking in the top 10%. However, single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is moderate, landing in the middle 50%, representing a tradeoff between massive parallel capacity and individual core speed.

This instance is highly suited for compute-heavy, parallelized workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and large-scale database operations, which all achieved top-tier benchmark results. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. Given the high density of 192 dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPUs, the c8i.48xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for enterprise-scale compute tasks, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations. While single-core performance is moderate, its support for advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512 enables exceptional parallel processing and cryptography capabilities. This instance is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including large-scale LLM prompt processing, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for high-performance parallel compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring 192 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing and high-throughput enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors.
384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix mathematics.
Optimized for parallel workloads, LLM prompt processing, and high-throughput web serving.
